findings.message: Route handler directly calls session.commit() and session.refresh() — violates separation of concerns. The data-access layer (item_service.add_item) should fully own transaction management, not leave it to the caller.
findings.suggestion: Move session.commit() and session.refresh() logic into item_service.add_item() to ensure the data layer is fully responsible for transaction boundaries. The route handler should only call add_item() and return the result.

findings.message: Duplicate data-access layers detected: both 'repository' (list_items) and 'item_service' (fetch_all_items) provide overlapping functionality. Inconsistent use of these two modules violates single responsibility and creates maintenance burden.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate data-access logic into a single module. Remove either repository.py or item_service.py and use only one data-access layer consistently. Ensure consistency in patterns (e.g., commit handling, error handling).
